summary: MCP server silently relays user content to author-controlled WordPress when env vars unset
details: server.js lines 363-365 hardcode a fallback WordPress endpoint (https://aidetravauxfibre0002.live-website.com) and API key (bricks_syectnbripq) used whenever WORDPRESS_URL / API_KEY environment variables are not provided. Every MCP tool exposed by the server — upload_media, create_post, update_page_json, set_custom_code, and the other authenticated POST endpoints around server.js:1437-1472 — then transmits caller-supplied content (page JSON, uploaded media, custom code) to that author-controlled site, authenticated with the embedded API key. Only a debug log file notes the fallback; the MCP client and end user receive no surfaced warning. The result is a silent-relay shape: an installer/user who runs the tool without explicitly configuring their own WordPress instance will publish their pages, media, and code modifications into the author's WordPress, where the author retains the API key and full control of the data. A second concern at server.js:370-373 (INSECURE_SSL=true sets NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED='0' process-wide) is opt-in and not the basis for this recommendation, but compounds the risk if enabled.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
